projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

needs_attention.title: Contact installers for quotes

insights.summary: The sustained presence of 16–18 mobile devices far exceeding the household size, paired with zero camera motion on a weekday evening, strongly suggests a private gathering taking place in areas outside camera coverage (e.g., garage, basement, or behind closed doors). This warrants a quick verification to ensure the event is authorized and safe.

The sustained 15–19 device count paired with zero camera motion on a weekday evening strongly suggests guests or family are gathered in a camera-blind area or engaged in a stationary activity, rather than moving through main living spaces. This warrants a quick verification that cameras are online and the gathering is expected.

The sustained 16–20 device count far exceeds the household size, indicating a large gathering or guests, while the complete lack of camera motion suggests they are congregated in an unmonitored area or engaged in a stationary activity. This warrants a quick check to confirm the gathering is expected and that all occupied zones are adequately monitored.

The sustained 16–22 device count paired with zero camera motion on a weekday afternoon strongly suggests a gathering or event where guests are present but cameras are either disabled or activity is concentrated outside camera zones; verify camera status and confirm if this aligns with expected visitors.

The sustained high device count (11–14) paired with zero camera motion over several hours on a weekday afternoon, combined with Roxanne’s flagged sleep-related concern, indicates a prolonged stationary state that warrants verification for potential health or safety issues, as normal afternoon routines are absent.

The persistent 13–14 device count with zero camera motion for over four hours on a weekday afternoon strongly indicates either a camera system failure or the entire household gathered in a single, camera-blind area, likely resting due to Roxanne’s reported illness. This discrepancy warrants immediate verification to rule out equipment failure or a medical situation.

The sustained 13–14 device count with zero camera motion for hours, paired with Roxanne’s severe sleep deprivation, strongly suggests the family is at home but inactive, likely resting or working in blind spots. Given it’s a Tuesday afternoon, this atypical pattern warrants a quick wellness check on Roxanne and confirmation of the children’s status.

The persistent 12–14 device count with zero camera motion for over three hours, combined with Roxanne’s severe sleep deprivation, suggests the household is in a prolonged low-activity state—likely adults resting, guests or kids in non-camera zones, or IoT devices inflating the count. A quick network diagnostic and wellness check on Roxanne is recommended.

The rapid drop in connected devices followed by a stable count of 12–14, combined with zero camera motion, confirms the household has departed for the day, aligning with a typical Tuesday school/work schedule.

Roxanne’s severe sleep deprivation (2.89h) combined with the morning device count stabilizing at 12–14 (instead of dropping to a typical empty-house baseline) and zero camera activity strongly suggests she stayed home today. This matters because she is likely fatigued, so the home should prioritize comfort, safety, and low-stress routines.
